description: Priority queue for the next wave of strategy candidates. Each entry is a Hypothesis Card — enough detail to move straight to smoke test without additional design work.

entries.rationale: EUR/USD at London open (08:20 ET) concentrates the highest FX volume of the day. The first 15-30 minutes establish a directional range driven by European economic data and overnight position unwinding. Breakout of that range has documented follow-through in academic literature (Cai, 2009; Osler, 2003).
entries.entry_logic: At session open (08:20 ET), accumulate high/low for orb_minutes=[15,30]. First close above range high → long entry; first close below range low → short entry. One trade per session only.
entries.exit_logic: Target = entry + rr_ratio × range_size. Stop = opposite side of range. Hard exit at 11:30 ET (London close) or EOD.
entries.risk_notes: Avoid days with 08:30 ET macro data (CPI, NFP, PPI) as range can be violated violently in both directions. Consider a news-filter flag.
entries.lookahead_risks: Opening range must be computed from bars strictly within the orb window — no current-bar close until the bar is complete.

entries.rationale: After the London open breakout, 30-40% of sessions see a 'fake-out' — the initial break reverses sharply. Entry on the reversal after a defined retrace captures the fade trade with defined risk.
entries.entry_logic: After a breakout signal (same range as LOB), if price retraces back INTO the range within 3 bars → enter opposite direction. Stop = the breakout high/low. Target = opposite side of range.
entries.exit_logic: Target = opposite side of opening range. Stop = breakout extreme. Exit at 11:30 ET.
entries.risk_notes: High failure rate on strong trend days. Regime filter (ADX > 25) should suppress signals on trending days.
entries.lookahead_risks: The 'fakeout' is defined post-hoc — ensure the entry signal bar is fully closed before acting.
entries.notes: Complement to london_open_breakout; should be tested AFTER LOB passes.

entries.rationale: VWAP is the institutional benchmark. When price dips below VWAP and quickly reclaims it with a strong close, institutional buyers stepped in. The reclaim bar signals continuation above VWAP.
entries.entry_logic: Bar closes below VWAP AND next bar closes above VWAP AND volume > 1.2× avg volume → long. Reverse for short. Only trade between 10:00 and 14:00 ET (avoid open volatility and EOD positioning).
entries.exit_logic: Target = prior swing high (for long). Stop = low of the reclaim bar. Max hold = 12 bars.
entries.risk_notes: Volume data quality is inconsistent in Databento 1-min CSV. Verify volume column is non-zero before using multiplier.
entries.lookahead_risks: VWAP must be computed cumulatively from session open — no look-back beyond current bar.
entries.notes: Requires session_vwap feature already added by ESDataLoader.add_features(). Check column name.

entries.rationale: Prior day high/low levels are widely watched by institutional desks. A sweep (brief breach) followed by a reversal back inside the prior range is one of the highest-probability intraday setups in futures (SMC literature; Sperandeo 1994).
entries.entry_logic: Price trades above prior_day_high by ≤ sweep_buffer ATR, then closes back below prior_day_high within sweep_bars → short. Reverse for low sweep. Entry at first close back inside.
entries.exit_logic: Target = prior_day_midpoint (high+low)/2. Stop = sweep extreme (the tick that breached). EOD exit.
entries.risk_notes: Do not trade if the open is ALREADY beyond the prior day range — the setup requires the sweep to happen during the session.
entries.lookahead_risks: Prior day high/low must use prior_close shift=1. Session_high/session_low are live and causal.
entries.notes: Similar to pivot_reaction.py but anchored to prior day, not S/R pivots.

entries.rationale: Extended low-volatility consolidation (ATR compression) reliably precedes explosive directional moves in commodity and FX markets. Entry on the breakout bar after N bars of below-average ATR.
entries.entry_logic: ATR(14) < ATR_slow(50) * compression_threshold for compression_bars consecutive bars. On the bar where ATR(14) expands above ATR_slow → enter in the direction of the bar (long if bullish bar, short if bearish). Filter: only enter if close is beyond the N-bar high (long) or N-bar low (short).
entries.exit_logic: Target = entry ± 2 × current ATR(14). Stop = prior N-bar low (for long). Max hold = 24 bars.
entries.risk_notes: False compression signals are common around economic releases. ATR spikes on a single bar can reset the streak counter incorrectly — use ATR smoothed over 3 bars.
entries.lookahead_risks: ATR(14) at bar close is causal — it uses close prices of completed bars only.
entries.notes: Energy and metal markets have better-defined volatility cycles than equity indices.

entries.rationale: ES futures regularly gap above/below the prior RTH close at the 09:30 open. Gaps under 0.5% fill within the session 60-70% of the time (Caporin et al. 2012). Entry on the open, target = prior close.
entries.entry_logic: At 09:30 open, if gap_pct > gap_min AND gap_pct < gap_max → enter against the gap direction. Gap_pct = (open - prior_close) / prior_close. Long if gap down, short if gap up.
entries.exit_logic: Target = prior_close (gap fill). Stop = open ± stop_atr × ATR(20). Exit by 11:00 ET if not filled.
entries.risk_notes: Do NOT trade gap fill on days following major macro events (NFP, FOMC, earnings gaps) — those gaps often extend. Consider a gap_max_pct upper bound to avoid chasing runaway moves.
entries.lookahead_risks: Gap calculation uses prior_close (shift=1) which is causal.
entries.notes: Requires gap_raw and gap_pct features from ESDataLoader.add_features().

entries.rationale: True Asian session (20:00-03:00 ET) range breakout for EUR. Requires loading overnight data, which is a pipeline change. High potential — this is the textbook London open breakout thesis.
entries.entry_logic: Define Asian range as high/low from 20:00-03:00 ET. At 03:00 ET (London open), if price breaks range → enter in breakout direction. Exit by 08:20 ET (M6E RTH start).
entries.exit_logic: Target = entry ± 1.5 × Asian range. Stop = opposite side of range.
entries.risk_notes: Pipeline MUST load overnight bars (no RTH filter). Currently blocked by ESDataLoader RTH-filter architecture. Resolve before testing.
entries.lookahead_risks: The Asian range uses bars from 20:00 to 03:00 only — no current session data.
entries.notes: BLOCKED: requires non-RTH data loading path. Implement after london_open_breakout_m6e is validated.

entries.rationale: bollinger_rsi_fxe was killed by conservative costs but had DSR=11.2 at realistic costs. The cost problem is well-understood: 9,453 trades × $0.0002 extra = ~$1.9 loss. A regime filter (ADX < 25 = range-bound) that reduces trade count by 30-40% while preserving the profitable range-bound trades should fix the cost cliff.
entries.entry_logic: Same as bollinger_rsi.py, AND ADX(14) < adx_threshold (range-bound filter). Reduces trade frequency on trending days where mean-reversion fails.
entries.exit_logic: Same as bollinger_rsi — BB midline target, ATR stop, 12-bar timeout.
entries.risk_notes: ADX feature must be added to ESDataLoader.add_features() before testing. Current pipeline does not compute ADX.
entries.lookahead_risks: ADX(14) at bar close is causal — uses completed bars only.
entries.notes: Direct rescue of a killed survivor. Should be Priority 1 if ADX feature is added to pipeline.

entries.rationale: Combining uncorrelated survivors (equity index + commodity + energy) with equal risk weighting consistently produces better Sharpe than any individual strategy. The 3 hardened survivors have very low pairwise correlation by asset class.
entries.entry_logic: Run all 3 strategies simultaneously. On each signal, size contracts so each trade risks the same dollar amount (equal risk per trade). Total capital = $75,000 (3 × $25k micro accounts or 1 $75k account).
entries.exit_logic: Each strategy manages its own exits independently.
entries.risk_notes: Requires simultaneous operation of 3 strategies. Platform must support multiple open positions. Topstep Combine allows only 1 strategy per account — this requires a funded account or prop arrangement.
entries.lookahead_risks: No portfolio-level lookahead if each strategy is individually causal.
entries.notes: Phase 4 (portfolio construction) in fortress workflow. Not for single-instrument paper trading.

entries.rationale: The FOMC drift on MES is a proven survivor. Gold and crude oil also exhibit pre-FOMC drift (defensive repositioning). EUR/USD shows the drift from the European session open the day before. Testing the same calendar effect on 3 additional markets is low-effort with high expected value.
entries.entry_logic: Same as fomc_drift.py but with instrument-specific entry/exit timing. MGC/MCL: enter at 08:20 ET (commodity session open day before FOMC). M6E: enter at 08:20 ET.
entries.exit_logic: Exit at 14:00 ET on FOMC day (same as MES version). Instrument-specific cost model.
entries.risk_notes: Rate-sensitive instruments (ZN, ZB) should NOT use this strategy as FOMC directly drives rate products — the drift edge may not exist or may be in the opposite direction.
entries.lookahead_risks: FOMC dates are known in advance and published by the Fed. No lookahead.
entries.notes: Multi-market extension of the existing fomc_drift survivor. Easy win if calendar effect holds cross-asset.
